entities.name: CROP PLANTS
entities.description: Cultivated plants used for agricultural production.

entities.name: WILD SPECIES
entities.description: Non-cultivated relatives of crop plants serving as a gene pool.

entities.name: GENE POOL
entities.description: The total set of genes available within a population or species.

entities.name: WIDE CROSSES
entities.description: The process of crossing wild taxa with cultivated crop plants.

entities.name: SEXUAL GENE TRANSFER
entities.description: The movement of genetic material between organisms via sexual reproduction.

entities.name: INCOMPATIBILITY
entities.description: Barriers that prevent successful interspecific hybridization and hybrid production.

entities.name: INTERSPECIFIC HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: The process of crossing two different species to create a hybrid.

entities.name: HYBRID PRODUCTION
entities.description: The creation of hybrid offspring from different taxa.

relationships.description: WILD SPECIES serve as a valuable GENE POOL
WIDE CROSSES are used to improve CROP PLANTS
WIDE CROSSES involve crossing WILD SPECIES with cultivated ones
INCOMPATIBILITY acts as a barrier to INTERSPECIFIC HYBRIDIZATION
INCOMPATIBILITY acts as a barrier to HYBRID PRODUCTION
INCOMPATIBILITY makes taxa unavailable for SEXUAL GENE TRANSFER
INTERSPECIFIC HYBRIDIZATION is a method for HYBRID PRODUCTION
